San Francisco Notice And Motion And Motion To Strike Petitioners Memorandum Of Costs Or In The Alternative To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Stephen Williams of Law Offices of Stephen M. Williams telephone number 415-292-3656 for Petitioner Neighbors for Preservation and Progress.
James Emery of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4628 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco.

The parties were provided with a tentative ruling in writing. The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF APPEALS, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and DEPARTMENT OF BUILDING INSPECTION'S Motion To Strike Petitioners Amended Memorandum Of Costs is denied and their Alternative Motion To Tax Certain Costs is granted in part. $3434 of the $10,243.47 sought in the amended costs memorandum is taxed, which means that petitioners shall recover $6,809.47 in costs from respondents. Petitioners' initial costs memorandum filed on August 17, 2015 was not untimely since it was filed within fifteen days of the statutorily-correct service of the notice of entry of judgment which occurred on August 7, 2015. The Court exercises its discretion to award costs to petitioners per CCP 1032(a)(4). Petitioners obtained non-monetary relief by the April 29, 2015 order granting their writ and vacating the Board of Appeals' February 26, 2014 decision. While petitioners were ultimately unsuccessful in their litigation objectives, a judgment was issued granting their writ and requiring the Board of Appeals to comply with the applicable law. This is sufficient to warrant the determination that petitioners are prevailing parties for purposes of the costs statutes. Items 1a, 1b, 1gii, 1giv,1gv, 1gvi, 1gviii and 1gix totaling $3434 are taxed because they are non-recoverable administrative fees. All other costs sought to be taxed are recoverable. Mr. Williams is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar May-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephen Williams of Law Offices of Stephen M. Willimas telephone number 415-292-3656 for Petitioner Neighbors for Preservation and Progress.
Carlos Alvarez of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP telephone number 415-421-3400 for Real Party In Interest Shizuo Holdings Trust/Mark Rutherford.
James Emery of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4628 for Respondent City and County of San Francisco.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER NEIGHBORS FOR PRESERVATION AND PROGRESS' Motion For Attorney's Fees pursuant to CCP 1021.5 is denied. Petitioner is not "'a successful party' which had initiated litigation that resulted in enforcement of an important public right and a significant benefit to the public." (Center for Biological Diversity v. California Fish and Game Commission (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 128, 131.) Judge Goldsmith rejected the majority of petitioner's arguments and remanded the matter for the limited purpose of having respondent explain its decision. Based on the record before Judge Goldsmith, it was unclear whether respondent relied on improper criteria when rendering its decision. As Judge Goldsmith explained in his order granting the writ of mandate at 3:21-4:2: When Respondent subsequently changed its draft decision and issued a final decision that allowed construction of the 5th floor, Respondent did not cite the HAA or explain whether it did or did not rely on the HAA to render its decision. The final decision refers to an analysis of the San Francisco Planning Code and General Plan as the basis for the decision. The final decision, however, does not appear to mirror the deliberative process which focused on the HAA. The Court grants the petition as Respondent failed to set forth the analytic route it traveled from evidence to action. See Topanga Assn. for a Scenic Comm., 11 Cal.3d at 515. Respondent and Real Party in Interest correctly note that petitioner attempts to re-characterize the purpose of the writ. The object of the petition was to reduce the size of the project because it was allegedly out-of-character with the neighborhood (Petitioner's Verified Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandate (CCP 1094.5) or Mandate (CCP 1085), 2:12-16; 3:23-4:5; 7:1-2). After the remand and re-hearing, there was no substantive change in Respondent's decision and the 5-story project moved forward. Lastly, the court finds that personal interests motivated this litigation and the cost of the legal victory did not transcend those personal interests. All of the directors and officers of petitioner reside on the same block as the project. (Heinecke Depo., 75; Zitrin Depo., 24-25). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
